attributes.name: ESXi Management Command Execution - vim-cmd, esxcli, vmdumper
attributes.description: Detects execution of ESXi management utilities (vim-cmd, esxcli, vmdumper) used by ESXi ransomware operations (RansomHub, Akira, Black Basta, LockBit) for VM enumeration, VM killing, snapshot deletion, and service disruption during hypervisor attacks.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and (process.name in ("vim-cmd", "esxcli", "vmdumper") or process.args in ("vmsvc/getallvms", "vm process kill", "vm process list", "vmsvc/power.off", "vmsvc/snapshot.removeall", "hostsvc/enable_ssh", "-l"))
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate ESXi administration by authorized VMware administrators
Scheduled maintenance scripts using vim-cmd or esxcli

attributes.name: Mass VM Process Kill - ESXi Ransomware Kill Sequence
attributes.description: Detects rapid VM force-kill operations via esxcli, characteristic of ESXi ransomware attacks. LockBit uses a 9x retry pattern for stubborn VMs (SQL, ERP), while RansomHub and Black Basta force-kill all running VMs before encrypting VMDK files. This threshold rule triggers when 3 or more kill commands are observed from the same host.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Planned VM migration during maintenance windows

attributes.name: SSH Key File Bulk Access - SSH-Snake Lateral Movement
attributes.description: Detects bulk access to SSH private key files, known_hosts, authorized_keys, and SSH config files indicating credential harvesting for lateral movement. SSH-Snake (Sysdig Jan 2024) self-propagates via harvested SSH credentials to compromise ESXi hosts. Triggers when a single process accesses 3 or more SSH key files.
attributes.query: event.category:file AND file.path:(*/.ssh/id_rsa* OR */.ssh/id_ed25519* OR */.ssh/id_ecdsa* OR */.ssh/authorized_keys* OR */.ssh/known_hosts* OR */.ssh/config OR */ssh_host_rsa_key* OR */ssh_host_ed25519_key*) AND NOT process.name:(sshd OR ssh-agent OR ssh-keygen)
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Ansible and automation tools reading SSH keys

attributes.name: ESX Admins AD Group Creation - CVE-2024-37085 Exploitation
attributes.description: Detects creation of Active Directory security groups matching the ESX Admins naming pattern. CVE-2024-37085 allows attackers to create this group to automatically gain full administrator access on domain-joined ESXi hosts without any ESXi-side authentication. Used by Storm-0506 and Black Basta. Any creation of this group should be investigated immediately.
attributes.query: iam where event.action in ("group-creation", "added-group-account") and group.name : ("ESX Admins", "ESXAdmins", "esxadmins", "ESX_Admins", "esxi admins")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate ESXi management group creation by authorized AD administrators

attributes.name: Rclone Data Exfiltration Activity - Cloud Storage Sync
attributes.description: Detects execution of Rclone or processes with Rclone-specific command line arguments targeting cloud storage providers (Mega, S3, SFTP). Rclone appears in 57% of ransomware incidents per ReliaQuest and is the preferred exfiltration tool for RansomHub, Akira, and Black Basta affiliates. Detects both the rclone binary name and Rclone-specific CLI arguments for renamed binary evasion.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and (process.name : "rclone" or process.args : ("mega:*", "s3:*", "sftp:*", "--transfers", "--checkers", "--s3-chunk-size", "rclone.conf"))
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate Rclone usage for backup operations

attributes.name: Ransomware File Extension Rename - VMDK Encryption Indicator
attributes.description: Detects file rename operations where files are renamed to known ransomware extensions (.ransomhub, .akira, .lockbit, .blackbasta, .encrypted). This indicates active ESXi ransomware encryption targeting VM disk files. Any single detection should trigger an immediate incident response.
attributes.query: file where event.type == "rename" and file.extension in ("ransomhub", "akira", "lockbit", "blackbasta", "encrypted", "enc")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: None expected for ransomware-specific file extensions

attributes.name: Ransom Note Deployment to Datastore Directories
attributes.description: Detects creation of ransom note files (README.txt, HOW_TO_DECRYPT.txt, RECOVER_YOUR_FILES.txt) across multiple directories. RansomHub deploys README.txt to all datastores and VM directories after encryption. Threshold set to 3 files from a single process to reduce false positives from legitimate README creation.
attributes.query: event.category:file AND event.action:creation AND file.name:(README.txt OR HOW_TO_DECRYPT.txt OR RECOVER_YOUR_FILES.txt OR DECRYPT_INSTRUCTIONS.txt OR RESTORE_FILES.txt)
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate README.txt creation in software projects -- unlikely in bulk

attributes.name: ESXi SSH Enable Followed by VM Kill - Multi-Stage Ransomware Sequence
attributes.description: Detects the characteristic ESXi ransomware attack sequence: SSH service enable followed by VM force-kill operations on the same host within a 2-hour window. This correlation provides high-confidence detection of an active hypervisor ransomware campaign. Extremely unlikely outside of authorized security testing.
attributes.query: sequence by host.id with maxspan=2h [process where event.type == "start" and process.args : ("hostsvc/enable_ssh", "hostsvc/start_ssh")] [process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "esxcli" and process.args : "vm process kill"]
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Extremely unlikely outside of authorized security testing

attributes.name: Binary Rename Evasion - Windows Process Name Masquerading on Linux
attributes.description: Detects execution of binaries named after legitimate Windows system processes (svchost.exe, csrss.exe, lsass.exe) on Linux systems. This is a common evasion technique where ransomware operators rename Rclone to a Windows process name. The presence of a .exe-named binary on Linux is inherently suspicious, especially when combined with data transfer arguments.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and host.os.type == "linux" and process.name : ("svchost.exe", "csrss.exe", "lsass.exe", "svhost.exe", "taskhost.exe")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: None expected -- Windows process names should never execute on Linux

attributes.name: VM Snapshot Deletion via vim-cmd - Recovery Prevention
attributes.description: Detects execution of vim-cmd snapshot removal commands which delete VM snapshots to prevent recovery. RansomHub and Black Basta delete all snapshots across all VMs before encryption to ensure victims cannot restore from snapshots. Combined with VM kill operations, this is a definitive indicator of ESXi ransomware.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate snapshot cleanup during planned maintenance

attributes.name: ESXi Critical Service Stop - Recovery Prevention
attributes.description: Detects stopping of critical ESXi services (hostd, vpxd, fdm, vsan) using init.d scripts. Ransomware operators stop these services to prevent VM restart, backup recovery, and management access during the encryption phase. Multiple service stops in rapid succession indicate an active ransomware attack.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.args : ("/etc/init.d/hostd stop", "/etc/init.d/vmware-vpxd stop", "/etc/init.d/vmware-fdm stop", "/etc/init.d/vmware-sps stop", "/etc/init.d/vsandevicemonitord stop", "/etc/init.d/rhttpproxy stop", "/etc/init.d/sfcbd-watchdog stop")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Planned ESXi maintenance with coordinated service restarts

attributes.name: Rclone Configuration File Creation - Exfiltration Setup
attributes.description: Detects creation of rclone.conf files or files within the .config/rclone directory. This indicates setup of cloud storage targets for data exfiltration. Ransomware operators create Rclone configs with Mega, S3, or SFTP targets before staging and exfiltrating victim data.
attributes.query: file where event.type == "creation" and (file.name : "rclone.conf" or file.path : "*/.config/rclone/*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate Rclone setup for backup operations

attributes.name: Free-Space Wiping via dd on Datastore - Anti-Forensics
attributes.description: Detects use of the dd command to write from /dev/zero or /dev/urandom to VMware datastore paths. LockBit 5.0 performs free-space wiping after encryption to overwrite deleted data and prevent forensic recovery. This is a definitive anti-forensics indicator when targeting /vmfs/volumes/ paths.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "dd" and process.args : ("if=/dev/zero", "if=/dev/urandom") and process.args : ("*/vmfs/volumes/*", "*.fswipe*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate disk sanitization during hardware decommissioning

attributes.name: nftables Manipulation - Possible CVE-2024-1086 Kernel Exploitation
attributes.description: Detects rapid nftables rule creation and deletion patterns that may indicate exploitation of CVE-2024-1086. This kernel vulnerability in nf_tables allows a use-after-free leading to privilege escalation. RansomHub and Akira affiliates use this exploit for root access on ESXi hosts.
attributes.query: process.name:nft AND process.args:("add chain" OR "add rule" OR "delete" OR "flush")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate firewall rule management by system administrators
